plexus.app/exam/case-15
The problem

OSCE prep is hard for reasons that have nothing to do with how much you know.

Real OSCE practice requires standardised patients, calibrated scoring, and consistent feedback. Most candidates get none of these. Plexus solves all three.

01

No one to practise with

Study partners are inconsistent, role-play groups are scarce, and clinical teachers don't have time for individual feedback. You end up rehearsing in your head, hoping it's enough.

02

Feedback that doesn't sting

Peer review is kind but vague. The feedback you actually need — what an examiner would mark you down for, why a missed red flag matters — is uncomfortable to give and rarely landed.

03

No way to know if you're ready

You finish a mock exam feeling fine, or panicked. Neither is a signal. Without consistent scoring across many attempts, you can't tell whether you're improving or trending the wrong way.

How it works

Three steps. Every case.

Plexus generates a fresh case for your chosen specialization, runs the consultation as an AI standardised patient, and gives you a Knowledge Bridge — a structured post-exam review — in under fifteen minutes.

1 Take the case

Drive the consultation. We respond as the patient.

Each case opens with a patient brief and supporting materials — voice recording, X-rays, ECGs, photographs where relevant. You lead the consultation by typing or speaking. The AI patient responds in character with realistic affect, history, and answers grounded in the case.

Voice input and text-to-speech for realistic encounters
Request physical examination findings; the AI confirms expected findings
Leave and resume any case; the transcript is always preserved
2 Get scored

Out of 100. Across five domains. Every time.

When you finish, the AI scores you against the OSCE rubric: clinical reasoning, safety, professionalism, communication, and structure & organisation. Each domain is rated out of 100 and weighted by your level, with explicit rationale for the score awarded.

Configurable pass threshold, set as a percentage to match your programme's standard
Domain-level breakdown so you see exactly where points were lost
Mandatory safety-critical feedback identifies missed red flags and unsafe decisions
3 Cross the bridge

The Knowledge Bridge turns a number into learning.

Every exam — pass or fail — generates a Knowledge Bridge: an active learning layer with the ideal answer framework, strengths to keep, missed key points, and a targeted reflective prompt. It's the difference between getting marked and getting better.

Ideal-answer framework shows the examiner's model approach
Targeted learning points — high-yield, actionable corrections
Reflective prompts that force active engagement with feedback
The full product

Ten screens. One product.

A complete walkthrough of every screen in the simulator — from the moment a candidate signs up to the audit log the platform administrators review at month-end.

Candidate-facing Screen 01 of 10

Exam chat

The hero workflow. The candidate runs a consultation with an AI standardised patient — typed or spoken — while the case materials sidebar shows the brief, persona, voice recording, and supporting imagery. Progress to the minimum five exchanges is visible at all times.

  • AI patient stays in character; never breaks role-play or hints
  • Voice input and TTS for realistic encounters; falls back to text if voice fails
  • Materials sidebar on desktop; slides in as a drawer on mobile
  • Cancel at any time; resume an in-progress case from the dashboard
Open this screen
plexus.app/exam/case-15
Candidate-facing Screen 02 of 10

Knowledge Bridge

The post-exam learning layer. Score out of 100 with pass-mark indicator, five-domain breakdown weighted by level with explicit rationale, safety findings called out separately, then strengths, missed points, ideal-answer framework, and a targeted reflective prompt.

  • Score ring with a configurable percentage pass threshold (default 65%)
  • Domain bars sum to total; reasoning is reproducible from the transcript
  • Safety-critical omissions surface above the fold — they matter most
  • Reflection prompt forces active processing, not passive reading
Open this screen
plexus.app/result/case-14
Candidate-facing Screen 03 of 10

Dashboard

The candidate home page. Readiness signal grounded in concrete metrics, ongoing exam slots (max 3), score trajectory across recent cases, and quick access to start a new exam or review past Knowledge Bridges.

  • Readiness state ("Approaching ready") bounded by passes-in-last-5 and trend
  • Three ongoing exam slots visible; blocking modal if a fourth is attempted
  • Score trajectory shows direction of travel — improvement is the real metric
  • First-time and populated states designed; smooth onboarding
Open this screen
plexus.app/dashboard
Candidate-facing Screen 04 of 10

Sign in & sign up

Passwordless authentication via magic link. Same flow for sign-up and sign-in — toggle between modes at the top. Specialisation picker shown on first account creation only.

  • No passwords to set, store, forget, or reset
  • Magic link expires after 15 minutes — three states designed (form / sent / expired)
  • Same-device magic link click logs the user in instantly
  • Brand panel on the left builds confidence before the email field
Open this screen
plexus.app/sign-in
Candidate-facing Screen 05 of 10

Profile & history

Account management plus the candidate's full exam history. Sub-tabs separate exam sessions from sign-in activity. A blocking overlay protects against accidental specialisation changes — switching tracks resets KPIs.

  • Edit name and email; email change requires re-verification via magic link
  • Specialisation change overlay — destructive action gated by explicit consent
  • Exam history with passes, fails, cancellations, and full session detail
  • Sign-in activity tracks every login, logout, and expired magic link
Open this screen
plexus.app/profile
Admin-facing Screen 06 of 10

Admin overview

The platform operator's landing page. KPIs across users, sessions, and token spend; a 30-day token consumption chart with budget overlay; and live system signals from the AI endpoints, database, and scheduled jobs.

  • At-a-glance health: 2,481 users, 347 active today, $3,840 spend MTD
  • Token chart segmented by model (Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5) against daily budget
  • System signals with severity levels for ops review
  • Side rail navigates to all admin sub-pages
Open this screen
plexus.app/admin/overview
Admin-facing Screen 07 of 10

Token consumption

The detailed view of AI spend. Filterable by day, week, or month; broken down by operation, by user, and by model. Anomaly indicators flag users whose consumption deviates from baseline.

  • Three time windows: 7d, 30d, 90d
  • By-user table with anomaly icons for unusual spikes
  • By-operation breakdown: case generation, simulation turns, scoring, KB generation
  • Export to CSV for finance reconciliation
Open this screen
plexus.app/admin/tokens
Admin-facing Screen 08 of 10

Users & specialisations

Two roles on one screen — a tabbed switch between the user roster (2,481 candidates) and the specialisations catalogue (6 active tracks). Each user is filterable by status; each specialisation can be edited or archived with a destructive-action confirmation.

  • Filter chips: All, Active, Blocked, Dormant, Deleted
  • Detail panel for each user — edit email, block by email/IP, unblock, delete
  • Specialisation deletion shows impact preview (X candidates affected)
  • Blocked-user view surfaces the Unblock action as the primary CTA
Open this screen
plexus.app/admin/users
Admin-facing Screen 09 of 10

System settings

The configuration room. The two most consequential decisions — the percentage pass threshold and max ongoing exams — sit prominently. Magic-link expiry, feature flags, and policy notes are grouped below. Every change is recorded with a reason note and pushed to the audit log.

  • Pass-threshold slider expressed as a percentage, configurable to your programme
  • Save button disabled until at least one change is made
  • Reason note required for any change to live scoring policy
  • Feature flags for voice input, TTS, and Knowledge Bridge generation
Open this screen
plexus.app/admin/settings
Admin-facing Screen 10 of 10

Audit log

Every administrative action recorded immutably. Filterable by admin, action type, and date range. Each event expands to show full before/after values plus actor metadata — IP, session, user agent. The view a compliance reviewer would open first.

  • Grouped by day with action-category pills (Policy, User mgmt, Access, Content, Destructive)
  • Inline expanded view shows the diff; detail panel shows full record
  • Stats strip: 847 events in 30d across 4 administrators
  • CSV export for compliance review and quarterly attestations
Open this screen
plexus.app/admin/audit

Built for serious preparation

Everything an exam-ready candidate needs.

A modern, focused environment for repeated practice — without timers, distractions, or theatre. Just the parts of OSCE prep that actually move your score.

Standardised-patient chat

The AI plays the patient — staying in character, withholding information until you ask, responding with realistic affect. No leading questions, no breaking role-play.

Rubric-based scoring

Versioned scoring prompts, four-domain rubric, and a configurable pass threshold your programme can set. Every score is reproducible from the transcript.

Safety-critical feedback

Missed red flags, unsafe decisions, and critical omissions are flagged explicitly. Patient-safety items influence the safety domain disproportionately.

Readiness tracking

An AI-driven readiness signal, bounded by concrete metrics (passes in last five, average score, trend). Not a vibe — an honest indicator.

Six OSCE station types

History-taking, assessment, counselling, clinical reasoning, emergency management, and functional assessment. Each case is classified, scored, and varied by type.

Voice in, voice out

Speak your responses, hear the patient's questions. Closer to the real OSCE environment than any pure-text simulator. Falls back to text if anything fails.

Specializations

Six tracks. More on the way.

Choose the specialization aligned to your finals. The case library, station mix, and rubric are calibrated per track. Switch tracks any time — your history is preserved.

A real case

What you actually see after an exam.

A real Knowledge Bridge from a chest-pain station: domain breakdown, safety findings, strengths, missed key points, and an ideal-answer framework. This is what you cross to learn from each case.

01 · Score Total out of 100, with the pass mark surfaced clearly. Gold ring on the pass-mark moment is intentional.
02 · Domains Four-domain breakdown with rationale, strengths, and gaps for each. The sum equals the total.
03 · Reflection A targeted reflective prompt forces active processing of the feedback. You don't just read — you reflect.
Practise anywhere

Built for phones, too.

The chat interface adapts to your device. Materials slide in as a drawer on mobile so you never lose the briefing or the X-ray while typing. Sign-in is passwordless — a single tap on the magic link.

Materials drawer slides in over the chat — never lose context
Voice input works seamlessly on iOS and Android
Sessions sync automatically — start on phone, finish on laptop

Frequently asked

Questions, answered.

No. Plexus is a complement to real practice with patients, peers, and clinical teachers — not a substitute. What it does is give you something most candidates lack: repeated, structured practice with consistent feedback, available whenever you have ten minutes. Pair it with bedside teaching and revision groups for the best results.
Plexus is built for anyone preparing for OSCE-style clinical assessments — medical students across all years, residents, and practicing physicians readying for oral examinations and clinical simulations. Cases adapt to your training level and chosen specialty, and the pass threshold is a configurable percentage so each programme can set it to its own standard. The case library covers six core specializations today, with more in development.
Each case includes a defined persona, history, affect, and case data. The AI plays the patient — it withholds information until you ask the right question, responds in character, and confirms expected examination findings when you request them. It will not lead you or hint at the diagnosis. The aim is realism, not collaboration.
Scoring follows a versioned four-domain rubric reviewed by qualified clinicians. Each domain has explicit weighting, rationale, and reproducible criteria. We continuously validate scoring against human-rated reference cases. Our scoring is meant to be directionally accurate — a reliable signal for self-assessment — not a substitute for examiner judgement.
Your transcripts, scores, and history are private to your account. We use anonymised aggregate data to improve scoring rubrics; we never share individual performance with third parties. You can delete your account at any time, which removes all personal data within 30 days.
During early access, Plexus is free for verified medical candidates with a 14-day full-access trial and no card required. Pricing for general availability has not yet been confirmed — sign up to be notified when plans are announced.
Plexus OSCE SIMULATOR

Repeated, realistic OSCE practice for medical students, residents, and physicians. Scored out of 100 across five clinical domains.
